claim: No Stable, release, conformance, package-parity, or recorded manual assistive-technology claim is made by this source contract.

semantics.nativeBasis: React Aria Slider with one visually hidden input type=range and one slider accessibility node
semantics.naming: A visible Field label, aria-label, or aria-labelledby names the group and thumb; thumbLabel can provide an explicit action name.
semantics.roleOverride: Consumer role overrides are outside the supported contract.
semantics.refTarget: HTMLDivElement visual root; the first hidden input receives the integrated Field control ID

interaction.keyboard: Arrow keys change one step, Page Up and Page Down change a larger page step, and Home and End reach the domain boundaries using React Aria semantics.
interaction.pointer: Primary track press and thumb drag update the same state; pointer cancellation ends the gesture without introducing another control path.
interaction.touch: The track and thumb preserve a 44 CSS pixel target and use pointer/touch movement without disabling keyboard operation.
interaction.speech: The visible Field label or explicit thumb label remains available for voice targeting.
interaction.switchAccess: The native range input is focusable and supports the complete keyboard path.

formBehavior: When name is supplied, the hidden native range input is a successful control with a canonical ASCII numeric value. Native reset restores the uncontrolled default; disabled sliders are omitted; read-only sliders remain successful.

states.synchronization: Output, thumb position, accessible value text, form value, read-only description, and change callbacks share one React Aria state engine.

preferences.enhancedContrast: Uses semantic tokens, geometry, and non-color invalid and read-only cues.
preferences.forcedColors: Uses system colors, explicit rail and thumb borders, and a system-color focus indicator.
preferences.reducedMotion: Thumb transitions are removed and no value change depends on animation.

responsive.overflowPolicy: The horizontal track shrinks to its container; intermediate mark labels hide on narrow canvases while endpoint context remains visible.

internationalization.imePolicy: The control does not accept free-form text and has no IME editing path.
internationalization.serialization: Visible and accessible values use Intl formatting; submitted values remain canonical JavaScript numbers.

consumerResponsibilities: Provide a persistent visible label and explain units when the formatted value is not self-evident.
Choose a discrete domain when exact values matter; use a NumberField when direct numeric entry is required.
Keep mark labels concise and treat them as visual context rather than the only explanation of allowed values.
Do not use read-only as a substitute for plain text when the value no longer needs slider semantics.
limitations: Source-present-unreleased records do not include immutable generated artifacts, packed-consumer evidence, package/source parity, or current manual assistive-technology sessions.
